steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: When two triangles are similar, corresponding angles are equal. The problem states that $\angle A$ corresponds to $\angle D$, so $\angle A = \angle D$.

steps.title: Apply the sine equality
steps.content:
Equal angles have equal sine values, so:
$$\sin(A)=\sin(D)$$
Since $\sin(A)=\frac{252}{277}$, it follows that $\sin(D)=\frac{252}{277}$. That confirms B is correct.
